questions.question: Based on paragraphs 10-12, what can you infer was important to Margaret as a photojournalist?

responses.content: One of Margaret’s first clients was the Otis Steel Company. Her success was due both to her technique and her skills in dealing with people. At first, several people at the company wondered if a woman could stand up to the intense heat and generally dirty and gritty conditions inside a steel mill. When Margaret finally got permission, the technical problems began.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight is too long. The longest acceptable answer is 222 characters, so the character limit is 289. The student's highlight is 389 characters, which exceeds the limit. Even though the highlight contains relevant content, it is automatically incorrect due to length.

responses.content: solved this problem by bringing along a new style of flare (which produces white light) and having assistants hold them to light her scenes. Her abilities resulted in some of the best steel factory pictures of that era, and these earned her national attention.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's most recent highlight is: "solved this problem by bringing along a new style of flare (which produces white light) and having assistants hold them to light her scenes. Her abilities resulted in some of the best steel factory pictures of that era, and these earned her national attention." This is within the character limit and directly answers the question by showing how Margaret's hardworking attitude (solving a technical problem) helped her advance her career (earning national attention). The highlight is a substring of approved answers and meets all requirements.
